task_updates.executor_notes: Extended dispatch for `post_topic` without changing summary storage (T4 remains pending). `_dispatch_tool_call` now routes `post_topic` to `_dispatch_create_topic_tool`; normalized block writeback now writes `args["blocks"]` for both `post_sectioned_topic` and `post_topic`; `action_by_tool` maps `post_topic` to `post_topic`; the blocks-required guard is split so `post_topic` rejects unconditionally on `not has_blocks` with `rejected_post_topic` / `post_topic_requires_blocks`, while legacy `post_sectioned_topic` preserves existing `not has_sections and not has_blocks` semantics and reason; the collision rejection map includes `post_topic: rejected_post_topic`; `build_rejected_transition` now accepts `rejected_post_topic`; idempotent collision override retry and tool input hint helper include `post_topic` so override retries and trace hints work for the new tool. Legacy `post_simple_topic` media/raw-media-ref and source/author-count guards remain in their existing order before the new split block-required guard. Syntax parse passed.

Rewrote `TOPIC_EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT`: role is now `BNDC live-update writer`; `post_topic` is named as the primary publishing tool; legacy `post_simple_topic` and `post_sectioned_topic` are listed once as deprecated/backward-compatible aliases; the media-to-sectioned rule was removed; editorial minimalism now states a single creator dropping one artifact is exactly one `intro` block with media attached, and warns against splitting one video into `The Video` / `Audio` / `Community Reaction` sections; brevity guidance now specifies intro body 1-3 sentences / roughly 30-150 words, section body 1-2 sentences, no bullet lists in posted prose, no padding, and no filler restatement of the title; a worked NebSH-style one-video example shows exactly one intro block with a `media_ref`, followed by a labeled `Don't do this` counter-example using three sections. Preserved the per-block source attribution rule, per-block media attachment rule, canonical media-ref shape, no-global-Sources-footer rule, block type guidance, and message_id-not-CDN guidance. Grep found no tests asserting the retired prompt strings (`BNDC live-update topic editor`, media-to-sectioned wording, or `post_sectioned_topic over`).

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: SC3 satisfied: writeback covers `post_sectioned_topic` and `post_topic`; action map includes `post_topic`; split guards are present with `post_topic` checking `not has_blocks` without consulting `has_sections`; collision map includes `rejected_post_topic`; `build_rejected_transition` accepts `rejected_post_topic`; legacy simple media/source-count guards remain before the new split blocks-required guard.

SC6 satisfied: prompt no longer contains old media-to-sectioned steering or the old editor role label; it contains the one-creator/one-artifact/minimum-blocks rule, brevity guidance, worked one-video intro-block example and labeled counter-example; source attribution, media attachment, canonical media_ref, and no-global-Sources-footer safety rails are preserved. Grep found no obsolete prompt-string tests to update.
